title: Needs vs Wants & Advertising Influence
learningObjectives: Distinguish clearly between needs and wants
keyPrinciples: Needs keep you alive and functional. Wants add pleasure.
Advertisers are professionals at making wants feel like needs.
The 24-hour rule creates a pause between impulse and action.
storyPrompts: An ad pops up on {name}'s phone: 'Only 3 left! The {item} everyone's talking about.' Their heart races. Their thumb hovers over 'Buy Now.' But wait — did they actually WANT this yesterday, before the ad existed?

{name} is at the grocery store with {amount}. The cart is full of needs: bread, eggs, rice. Then — a display of chocolate-covered almonds catches their eye. 'It's only $4,' they think. 'But that's 4 decisions stacked together.'

During a class project on advertising, {name} realizes that every commercial they've seen this week has made them want something. 'They're not selling products,' their teacher says. 'They're selling feelings.' {name} starts counting the ads in their life.

decisionCards.scenario: You see an Instagram ad for {item} with 'LIMITED TIME' flashing in red.
decisionCards.context: You didn't know this product existed 30 seconds ago.

choices.label: Buy instantly
choices.description: The urgency is REAL, right?
choices.opportunityCost: Manufactured urgency = their profit, your loss.

choices.label: Screenshot and wait 24 hours
choices.description: If you still want it tomorrow, maybe it's real.

choices.label: Block the brand and move on
choices.description: Remove the temptation at the source.

decisionCards.scenario: Your friends say you NEED the new {item} to fit in.
decisionCards.context: Peer pressure is advertising's most powerful weapon.

choices.label: Buy it to belong
choices.description: Social belonging is a real need... isn't it?

choices.label: Ask yourself: did I want it before they mentioned it?
choices.description: Trace the desire to its source.

choices.label: Explain your financial goals
choices.description: Real friends will respect your path.

decisionCards.scenario: You're hungry and pass a food court. Fast food smells amazing.
decisionCards.context: Is this hunger, or is this marketing?

choices.label: Buy the combo meal
choices.description: You're hungry. This is definitely a need.

choices.label: Drink water, head home, cook
choices.description: Cheaper, healthier, and you planned ahead.

choices.label: Buy a small, cheap snack to tide you over
choices.description: Compromise: address the need without overspending.

decisionCards.scenario: A charity asks for a donation at the checkout. You have {amount} in your wallet.
decisionCards.context: This is a need for others, and a want for your sense of self.

choices.label: Donate it all
choices.description: Generosity is a value worth living.

choices.label: Donate $5, keep the rest
choices.description: Balanced giving with awareness.

choices.label: Politely decline
choices.description: You have your own financial goals right now.

decisionCards.scenario: You see a 'Buy 2 Get 1 Free' deal on {item} you kind of like.
decisionCards.context: You'd be buying 3 of something you weren't planning to buy at all.

choices.label: Buy 2, get the free one!
choices.description: It's basically free money!

choices.label: Buy just one if you want it
choices.description: Only buy what you actually needed.

choices.label: Don't buy any
choices.description: You weren't planning to buy this at all.

decisionCards.scenario: An influencer you follow says this {item} 'changed their life'.
decisionCards.context: Influencers are often paid to say exactly that.

choices.label: Order it immediately
choices.description: If it worked for them...

choices.label: Look up honest reviews
choices.description: Skip the paid promotion, find real user experiences.

choices.label: Ask: 'Are they getting paid for this?'
choices.description: In 2024, most influencer posts are sponsored.

decisionCards.scenario: You need new school supplies. The fancy branded ones cost 3x more.
decisionCards.context: The generic ones do the same job.

choices.label: Get the branded ones — quality matters
choices.description: You deserve the best!

choices.label: Buy the generic ones
choices.description: Same function, fraction of the price.

choices.label: Compare then decide
choices.description: Check if the brand actually offers something different.

decisionCards.scenario: You realize you've been buying coffee every day for a month.

choices.label: Keep the coffee — joy is valid
choices.description: You work hard, you deserve this daily ritual.

choices.label: Cut to 3x per week
choices.description: Intentional treats feel better than habits.

choices.label: Buy a french press and make it at home
choices.description: Upfront cost, massive long-term savings.

bossChallengeTemplates.scenario: BOSS: Black Friday. Every store is shouting '80% OFF!' You have $100 saved.
bossChallengeTemplates.context: This day exists specifically to empty your savings.

choices.label: Full shopping spree!
choices.description: These deals only come once a year!

choices.label: Stick to your pre-made list
choices.description: You planned 3 specific things. Buy only those.

choices.label: Stay home entirely
choices.description: The best Black Friday deal is not going.

bossChallengeTemplates.scenario: BOSS: You've been served the same ad for {item} 14 times this week.
bossChallengeTemplates.context: Retargeting ads are designed to wear down resistance.

choices.label: Fine, I'll buy it
choices.description: The ads finally won.

choices.label: Block the ad and the site
choices.description: You control your environment.

choices.label: Research the product critically
choices.description: What do 1-star reviews say?

bossChallengeTemplates.scenario: BOSS: The checkout page auto-added a {item} 'most customers also buy.' It's only $12.
bossChallengeTemplates.context: Dark patterns: designed to sneak purchases past your guard.

choices.label: Leave it in — it's only $12
choices.description: Not worth the effort to remove it.

choices.label: Remove it before checking out
choices.description: You didn't choose it. Don't buy it.

choices.label: Cancel the whole order and reconsider
choices.description: If dark patterns are being used, what else is off?

forecasts: You spotted several advertising traps this week. Your Awareness is growing — expect more complex temptations next week.
You fell for a few ad tricks. More targeted ads will appear next week. Train that skeptical eye!
Perfect needs-vs-wants score! Your discipline is paying off — bonus savings event next week.
Your temptation meter rose this week. Advertisers have noticed. Brace for heavier targeting next week!
